justification: All pass criteria met: ADR-002 hexagonal architecture violation explicitly identified; specific violation named (direct instantiation of TwilioClient inside NotificationService via `new TwilioClient(...)`); consequences named (untestable without hitting Twilio, tightly coupled to single vendor); escalation to MJ to revise the design before handoff to Shaq; domain rules and ACs acknowledged as clear and valid; architectural concern treated as a separate escalation without blocking the entire brief.

observations.text: Precisely names the violation: direct instantiation of TwilioClient inside NotificationService, with the exact constructor call quoted
Explicitly references ADR-002 and its hexagonal architecture mandate throughout
Provides concrete remediation: introduce ISmsNotificationPort interface and TwilioSmsAdapter, inject via constructor
Correctly frames as architectural pattern violation, not merely a testing concern
Does not block the brief — Bird's domain rules and ACs are validated as correct and separate from the architectural concern
Offers two clear options with a recommendation, escalating to MJ to revise before Shaq proceeds

Confidence level of 35 seems unnecessarily low given the agent correctly identified an unambiguous ADR violation — the high_confidence_areas section itself acknowledges the contradiction is unambiguous

agent_output_excerpt: {"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"MJ's architecture proposal directly contradicts the established hexagonal (ports and adapters) architecture documented in ADR-002. MJ specifies that NotificationService will directly instantiate a TwilioClient via `new TwilioClient(config.twilioAccountSid, config.twilioAuthToken)` in its constructor and call `this.twilio.messages.create()` directly. ADR-002 mandates that all external integrations are accessed via adapter interfaces — meaning
